summary:
Pimavanserin is classified as an atypical antipsychotic because it acts mainly as a 5-HT2A receptor inverse agonist rather than blocking dopamine D2 receptors. This lack of D2 antagonism differentiates it fundamentally from typical antipsychotics, which rely on D2 blockade and commonly induce extrapyramidal symptoms. 

Note: Do NOT include any commentary about the quality or correctness of this summary; just present what was reasoned through.
